name: py-zabbix

metadata.name: py-zabbix
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v2 (GPLv2)
metadata.summary: Python module to work with zabbix.

documentation:
|Build Status| |Coverage| |PyPi status| |PyPi version|

Zabbix module for Python
========================

Install
-------

You can install Zabbix modules for Python with pip:

::

    pip install py-zabbix

Official documentation for `py-zabbix <https://py-zabbix.readthedocs.org/en/latest/>`__
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Examples
--------

ZabbixAPI
~~~~~~~~~

.. code:: python

    from pyzabbix.api import ZabbixAPI

    # Create ZabbixAPI class instance
    zapi = ZabbixAPI(url='https://localhost/zabbix/', user='Admin', password='zabbix')

    # Get all monitored hosts
    result1 = zapi.host.get(monitored_hosts=1, output='extend')

    # Get all disabled hosts
    result2 = zapi.do_request('host.get',
                              {
                                  'filter': {'status': 1},
                                  'output': 'extend'
                              })

    # Filter results
    hostnames1 = [host['host'] for host in result1]
    hostnames2 = [host['host'] for host in result2['result']]

    # Logout from Zabbix
    zapi.user.logout()

Or use 'with' statement to logout automatically:

.. code:: python

    from pyzabbix.api import ZabbixAPI

    # Create ZabbixAPI class instance
    with ZabbixAPI(url='https://localhost/zabbix/', user='Admin', password='zabbix') as zapi:

        # Get all monitored hosts
        result1 = zapi.host.get(monitored_hosts=1, output='extend')

Enable logging:

.. code:: python

    import sys
    import logging
    from pyzabbix.api import ZabbixAPI

    # Create ZabbixAPI class instance
    logger = logging.getLogger("pyzabbix")
    logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG)
    handler = logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout)
    logger.addHandler(handler)

    zapi = ZabbixAPI(url='http://localhost', user='Admin', password='zabbix')

Note that passwords and auth tokens are hidden when raw messages are logged or raised in exceptions ( but not hidden if print() is used):

.. code:: python

    ZabbixAPI.login(Admin,********)
    Call user.login method
    urllib2.Request(http://localhost/api_jsonrpc.php, {"jsonrpc": "2.0", "method": "user.login", "params": {"user": "Admin", "password": "********"}, "id": "1"})
    Response Body: {
        "jsonrpc": "2.0",
        "result": "********",
        "id": "1"
    }


ZabbixSender
~~~~~~~~~~~~

.. code:: python

    from pyzabbix import ZabbixMetric, ZabbixSender

    # Send metrics to zabbix trapper
    packet = [
      ZabbixMetric('hostname1', 'test[cpu_usage]', 2),
      ZabbixMetric('hostname1', 'test[system_status]', "OK"),
      ZabbixMetric('hostname1', 'test[disk_io]', '0.1'),
      ZabbixMetric('hostname1', 'test[cpu_usage]', 20, 1411598020),
    ]

    result = ZabbixSender(use_config=True).send(packet)

.. |Build Status| image:: https://travis-ci.org/adubkov/py-zabbix.svg?branch=master
   :target: https://travis-ci.org/adubkov/py-zabbix
.. |Coverage| image:: https://coveralls.io/repos/github/adubkov/py-zabbix/badge.svg?branch=master
   :target: https://coveralls.io/github/adubkov/py-zabbix?branch=master

```pycon
>>> from pyzabbix import ZabbixAPI
>>> z = ZabbixAPI('https://zabbix.server', user='Admin', password='zabbix')
>>> # Get API Version
>>> z.api_info.version()
>>> u'2.2.1'
>>> # or
>>> z.do_request('apiinfo.version')
>>> {u'jsonrpc': u'2.0', u'result': u'2.2.1', u'id': u'1'}
>>> # Get all disabled hosts
>>> z.host.get(status=1)
>>> # or
>>> z.do_request('host.getobjects', {'status': 1})
```

classes.name: pyzabbix.sender.ZabbixMetric

methods.name: pyzabbix.sender.ZabbixMetric.__repr__
documentation: Represent detailed ZabbixMetric view.

The {class}`ZabbixMetric` contain one metric for zabbix server.

```
type host

str

param host

Hostname as it displayed in Zabbix.

type key

str

param key

Key by which you will identify this metric.

type value

str

param value

Metric value.

type clock

int

param clock

Unix timestamp. Current time will used if not specified.
```

```pycon
>>> from pyzabbix import ZabbixMetric
>>> ZabbixMetric('localhost', 'cpu[usage]', 20)
```

methods.name: pyzabbix.sender.ZabbixSender._load_from_config
documentation:
Load zabbix server IP address and port from zabbix agent config
file.

If ServerActive variable is not found in the file, it will
use the default: 127.0.0.1:10051

```
type config_file

str

param use_config

Path to zabbix_agentd.conf file to load settings
from. If value is  then default config path will used:
/etc/zabbix/zabbix_agentd.conf
```

methods.name: pyzabbix.sender.ZabbixSender.send
documentation:
Send the metrics to zabbix server.

```
type metrics

list

param metrics

List of  to send
to Zabbix

rtype



return

Parsed response from Zabbix Server
```

The {class}`ZabbixSender` send metrics to Zabbix server.

Implementation of
[zabbix protocol](https://www.zabbix.com/documentation/1.8/protocols).

```
type zabbix_server

str

param zabbix_server

Zabbix server ip address. Default: 

type zabbix_port

int

param zabbix_port

Zabbix server port. Default: 

type use_config

str

param use_config

Path to zabbix_agentd.conf file to load settings from.
If value is  then default config path will used:
/etc/zabbix/zabbix_agentd.conf

type chunk_size

int

param chunk_size

Number of metrics send to the server at one time

type socket_wrapper

function

param socket_wrapper

to provide a socket wrapper function to be used to
wrap the socket connection to zabbix.
Example:

source:24: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

from pyzabbix import ZabbixSender
import ssl
secure_connection_option = dict(..)
zs = ZabbixSender(

source:28: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

zabbix_server=zabbix_server,
zabbix_port=zabbix_port,
socket_wrapper=lambda sock:ssl.wrap_socket(sock,**secure_connection_option)

source:31: (WARNING/2) Block quote ends without a blank line; unexpected unindent.

source:31: (INFO/1) Unexpected possible title overline or transition.
Treating it as ordinary text because it's so short.

)

type timeout

int

param timeout

Number of seconds before call to Zabbix server times out
Default: 10
```

```pycon
>>> from pyzabbix import ZabbixMetric, ZabbixSender
>>> metrics = []
>>> m = ZabbixMetric('localhost', 'cpu[usage]', 20)
>>> metrics.append(m)
>>> zbx = ZabbixSender('127.0.0.1')
>>> zbx.send(metrics)
```
